translations.idiomatic: Rain, drought, cold, and heat all take wind as root; when all four qi are disordered, therefore the punishment is constant wind.

translations.idiomatic: Constant wind harms things; therefore its extreme is violent death, shortening, and breakage.

translations.idiomatic: Another view: if greasy substances at night become anomalous, like greasy water staining clothes at night, it symbolizes licentiousness.

translations.idiomatic: Another view: night anomalies are when clouds and wind rise together into darkness, therefore sharing the same image as constant wind.

translations.idiomatic: Warmth plus wind generates borers and locusts; there are prodigies of naked insects.

translations.idiomatic: Another view: when earth qi is excessive, flowers bloom again in autumn and winter.

translations.idiomatic: In the Changes Kun is earth and ox. Oxen have large hearts yet cannot deliberate; when thought-mind qi is damaged, therefore there are ox calamities.

translations.idiomatic: Earth's color is yellow; therefore there are yellow blights and yellow portents.

translations.idiomatic: All damage to thought-mind is illness of earth qi; when earth qi is ill, metal, wood, water, and fire afflict it. Therefore it says "then at times there are metal, wood, water, and fire afflicting earth."

translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says: "It was wind."

translations.idiomatic: The Classic records what was seen, therefore it records their retreating flight;

translations.chinese: 象宋襄公區霿自用，不容臣下，逆司馬子魚之諫，而與彊楚爭盟，後六年為楚所執，應六鶂之數云。
translations.literal: Literal: It symbolized Duke Xiang of Song's obscured self-will, not tolerating ministers below, rejecting Marshal Ziyu's remonstrance, and competing with strong Chu for alliance. Six years later he was captured by Chu, corresponding to the number six of the yi birds.
translations.idiomatic: It symbolized Duke Xiang of Song's obscured self-will, not tolerating ministers below, rejecting Marshal Ziyu's remonstrance, and competing with strong Chu for alliance. Six years later he was captured by Chu, corresponding to the number six of the yi birds.

translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Hidden dragon, do not act. When the masses rebel with one will and highest virtue is hidden, its anomaly is wind.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Hidden dragon, do not act. When the masses rebel with one will and highest virtue is hidden, its anomaly is wind.

context.previous: 象宋襄公區霿自用，不容臣下，逆司馬子魚之諫，而與彊楚爭盟，後六年為楚所執，應六鶂之數云。

translations.literal: Literal: Its wind moves without easing things, gives no growth, and rain is slight yet harmful.
translations.idiomatic: Its wind moves without easing things, gives no growth, and rain is slight yet harmful.

context.previous: 京房易傳曰：「潛龍勿用，眾逆同志，至德乃潛，厥異風。
context.next: 政悖德隱茲謂亂，厥風先風不雨，大風暴起，發屋折木。

translations.chinese: 政悖德隱茲謂亂，厥風先風不雨，大風暴起，發屋折木。
translations.literal: Literal: When governance is perverse and virtue concealed, this is called disorder: first wind without rain, then great wind suddenly rising, tearing roofs and breaking trees.
translations.idiomatic: When governance is perverse and virtue concealed, this is called disorder: first wind without rain, then great wind suddenly rising, tearing roofs and breaking trees.

context.previous: 其風也，行不解物，不長，雨小而傷。
context.next: 守義不進茲謂耄，厥風與雲俱起，折五穀莖。

translations.chinese: 守義不進茲謂耄，厥風與雲俱起，折五穀莖。
translations.literal: Literal: When one guards righteousness yet does not advance, this is called senility: its wind rises with clouds, snapping stalks of the five grains.
translations.idiomatic: When one guards righteousness yet does not advance, this is called senility: its wind rises with clouds, snapping stalks of the five grains.

context.previous: 政悖德隱茲謂亂，厥風先風不雨，大風暴起，發屋折木。

translations.chinese: 臣易上政，茲謂不順，厥風大焱發屋。
translations.literal: Literal: When ministers alter the ruler's government, this is called noncompliance: its wind is blazing and tears roofs.
translations.idiomatic: When ministers alter the ruler's government, this is called noncompliance: its wind is blazing and tears roofs.

context.previous: 守義不進茲謂耄，厥風與雲俱起，折五穀莖。
context.next: 賦斂不理茲謂禍，厥風絕經紀，止即溫，溫即蟲。

translations.chinese: 賦斂不理茲謂禍，厥風絕經紀，止即溫，溫即蟲。
translations.literal: Literal: When taxation and levies are mismanaged, this is called calamity: its wind cuts off order; when it stops there is warmth, and warmth brings insects.
translations.idiomatic: When taxation and levies are mismanaged, this is called calamity: its wind cuts off order; when it stops there is warmth, and warmth brings insects.

context.previous: 臣易上政，茲謂不順，厥風大焱發屋。
context.next: 侯專封茲謂不統，厥風疾，而樹不搖，穀不成。

translations.literal: Literal: When feudal lords control enfeoffments, this is called lack of unity: its wind is swift yet trees do not sway and grain does not ripen.
translations.idiomatic: When feudal lords control enfeoffments, this is called lack of unity: its wind is swift yet trees do not sway and grain does not ripen.

context.previous: 賦斂不理茲謂禍，厥風絕經紀，止即溫，溫即蟲。
context.next: 辟不思道利，茲謂無澤，厥風不搖木，旱無雲，傷禾。

translations.chinese: 辟不思道利，茲謂無澤，厥風不搖木，旱無雲，傷禾。
translations.literal: Literal: When rulers do not think of the way of benefit, this is called lack of grace: its wind does not move trees, drought has no clouds, and crops are harmed.
translations.idiomatic: When rulers do not think of the way of benefit, this is called lack of grace: its wind does not move trees, drought has no clouds, and crops are harmed.

context.previous: 侯專封茲謂不統，厥風疾，而樹不搖，穀不成。
context.next: 公常於利茲謂亂，厥風微而溫，生蟲蝗，害五穀。

translations.chinese: 公常於利茲謂亂，厥風微而溫，生蟲蝗，害五穀。
translations.literal: Literal: When the duke constantly seeks profit, this is called disorder: its wind is slight and warm, breeding locusts and insects that harm the five grains.
translations.idiomatic: When the duke constantly seeks profit, this is called disorder: its wind is slight and warm, breeding locusts and insects that harm the five grains.

context.previous: 辟不思道利，茲謂無澤，厥風不搖木，旱無雲，傷禾。
context.next: 棄正作淫茲謂惑，厥風溫，螟蟲起，害有益人之物。

translations.chinese: 棄正作淫茲謂惑，厥風溫，螟蟲起，害有益人之物。
translations.literal: Literal: Abandoning the proper and practicing licentiousness is called delusion: its wind is warm, borers arise, and useful things to people are harmed.
translations.idiomatic: Abandoning the proper and practicing licentiousness is called delusion: its wind is warm, borers arise, and useful things to people are harmed.

context.previous: 公常於利茲謂亂，厥風微而溫，生蟲蝗，害五穀。
context.next: 侯不朝茲謂叛，厥風無恆，地變赤而殺人。」

translations.chinese: 侯不朝茲謂叛，厥風無恆，地變赤而殺人。」
translations.literal: Literal: When feudal lords do not come to court, this is called rebellion: its wind is erratic, the earth turns red, and people die."
translations.idiomatic: When feudal lords do not come to court, this is called rebellion: its wind is erratic, the earth turns red, and people die."

context.previous: 棄正作淫茲謂惑，厥風溫，螟蟲起，害有益人之物。

translations.chinese: 文帝二年六月，淮南王都壽春大風毀民室，殺人。
translations.literal: Literal: In sixth month of Emperor Wen year two, in Shouchun, capital of the Huainan king, great wind destroyed commoners' houses and killed people.
translations.idiomatic: In sixth month of Emperor Wen year two, in Shouchun, capital of the Huainan king, great wind destroyed commoners' houses and killed people.

context.next: 劉向以為是歲南越反，攻淮南邊，淮南王長破之，後年入朝，殺漢故丞相辟陽侯，上赦之，歸聚姦人謀逆亂，自稱東帝，見異不寤，後遷于蜀，道死廱。

translations.chinese: 劉向以為是歲南越反，攻淮南邊，淮南王長破之，後年入朝，殺漢故丞相辟陽侯，上赦之，歸聚姦人謀逆亂，自稱東帝，見異不寤，後遷于蜀，道死廱。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang held that that year Nanyue rebelled and attacked Huainan's border; King Chang of Huainan broke them. The next year he came to court, killed former Han chancellor Marquis of Biyang, was pardoned by the throne, then returned and gathered evil men to plot rebellion, calling himself Eastern Emperor. Seeing anomalies he did not awaken; later he was moved to Shu and died on the road in Yong.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that that year Nanyue rebelled and attacked Huainan's border; King Chang of Huainan broke them. The next year he came to court, killed former Han chancellor Marquis of Biyang, was pardoned by the throne, then returned and gathered evil men to plot rebellion, calling himself Eastern Emperor. Seeing anomalies he did not awaken; later he was moved to Shu and died on the road in Yong.

context.previous: 文帝二年六月，淮南王都壽春大風毀民室，殺人。

translations.chinese: 文帝五年，吳暴風雨，壞城官府民室。
translations.literal: Literal: In Emperor Wen year five, in Wu there were violent winds and rains, damaging city walls, offices, and homes.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Wen year five, in Wu there were violent winds and rains, damaging city walls, offices, and homes.

context.next: 時吳王濞謀為逆亂，天戒數見，終不改寤，後卒誅滅。

translations.chinese: 時吳王濞謀為逆亂，天戒數見，終不改寤，後卒誅滅。
translations.literal: Literal: At that time King Pi of Wu plotted rebellion. Heaven's warnings appeared repeatedly, yet he never reformed, and in the end he was executed and destroyed.
translations.idiomatic: At that time King Pi of Wu plotted rebellion. Heaven's warnings appeared repeatedly, yet he never reformed, and in the end he was executed and destroyed.

context.previous: 文帝五年，吳暴風雨，壞城官府民室。

translations.chinese: 五年十月，楚王都彭城大風從東南來，毀巿門，殺人。
translations.literal: Literal: In tenth month of year five, in Pengcheng, capital of the King of Chu, a great wind came from the southeast, destroyed market gates, and killed people.
translations.idiomatic: In tenth month of year five, in Pengcheng, capital of the King of Chu, a great wind came from the southeast, destroyed market gates, and killed people.

context.next: 是月王戊初嗣立，後坐淫削國，與吳王謀反，刑僇諫者。

translations.chinese: 是月王戊初嗣立，後坐淫削國，與吳王謀反，刑僇諫者。
translations.literal: Literal: That month King Wu had just inherited the throne; later, convicted of licentiousness, his state was reduced. He plotted rebellion with the King of Wu and executed remonstrants.
translations.idiomatic: That month King Wu had just inherited the throne; later, convicted of licentiousness, his state was reduced. He plotted rebellion with the King of Wu and executed remonstrants.

context.previous: 五年十月，楚王都彭城大風從東南來，毀巿門，殺人。
context.next: 吳在楚東南，天戒若曰，勿與吳為惡，將敗巿朝。

translations.chinese: 吳在楚東南，天戒若曰，勿與吳為惡，將敗巿朝。
translations.literal: Literal: Wu lay southeast of Chu. Heaven's warning was as if saying: do not do evil with Wu, or your markets and court will be ruined.
translations.idiomatic: Wu lay southeast of Chu. Heaven's warning was as if saying: do not do evil with Wu, or your markets and court will be ruined.

context.previous: 是月王戊初嗣立，後坐淫削國，與吳王謀反，刑僇諫者。
context.next: 王戊不寤，卒隨吳亡。

translations.chinese: 王戊不寤，卒隨吳亡。
translations.literal: Literal: King Wu did not awaken and eventually perished with Wu.
translations.idiomatic: King Wu did not awaken and eventually perished with Wu.

context.previous: 吳在楚東南，天戒若曰，勿與吳為惡，將敗巿朝。

translations.chinese: 釐公十五年「九月己卯晦，震夷伯之廟」。
translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Xi year fifteen: "On the last day, jimao, of the ninth month, thunder struck the temple of Yi Bo."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi year fifteen: "On the last day, jimao, of the ninth month, thunder struck the temple of Yi Bo."

context.next: 劉向以為晦，暝也；

translations.chinese: 劉向以為晦，暝也；
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang held that "last day" means darkness;
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that "last day" means darkness;

context.previous: 釐公十五年「九月己卯晦，震夷伯之廟」。
context.next: 震，雷也。

translations.chinese: 震，雷也。
translations.literal: Literal: "zhen" means thunder.
translations.idiomatic: "zhen" means thunder.

context.previous: 劉向以為晦，暝也；
context.next: 夷伯，世大夫，正書雷，其廟獨冥。

translations.chinese: 夷伯，世大夫，正書雷，其廟獨冥。
translations.literal: Literal: Yi Bo was a hereditary grandee; the record properly writes thunder, and his temple alone was darkened.
translations.idiomatic: Yi Bo was a hereditary grandee; the record properly writes thunder, and his temple alone was darkened.

context.previous: 震，雷也。
context.next: 天戒若曰，勿使大夫世官，將專事暝晦。

translations.chinese: 天戒若曰，勿使大夫世官，將專事暝晦。
translations.literal: Literal: Heaven's warning was as if saying: do not let grandees hold hereditary office, or they will monopolize affairs in darkness.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was as if saying: do not let grandees hold hereditary office, or they will monopolize affairs in darkness.

context.previous: 夷伯，世大夫，正書雷，其廟獨冥。
context.next: 明年，公子季友卒，果世官，政在季氏。

translations.chinese: 明年，公子季友卒，果世官，政在季氏。
translations.literal: Literal: The next year Prince Jiyou died; hereditary office indeed continued, and power rested with the Ji clan.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Prince Jiyou died; hereditary office indeed continued, and power rested with the Ji clan.

context.previous: 天戒若曰，勿使大夫世官，將專事暝晦。
context.next: 至成公十六年「六月甲午晦」，正晝皆暝，陰為陽，臣制君也。

translations.chinese: 至成公十六年「六月甲午晦」，正晝皆暝，陰為陽，臣制君也。
translations.literal: Literal: By Duke Cheng year sixteen, "on jiawu day at month's end in sixth month," broad daylight became dark: yin as yang, ministers controlling the ruler.
translations.idiomatic: By Duke Cheng year sixteen, "on jiawu day at month's end in sixth month," broad daylight became dark: yin as yang, ministers controlling the ruler.

context.previous: 明年，公子季友卒，果世官，政在季氏。
context.next: 成公不寤，其冬季氏殺公子偃。

translations.chinese: 成公不寤，其冬季氏殺公子偃。
translations.literal: Literal: Duke Cheng did not awaken; that winter the Ji clan killed Prince Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Cheng did not awaken; that winter the Ji clan killed Prince Yan.

context.previous: 至成公十六年「六月甲午晦」，正晝皆暝，陰為陽，臣制君也。
context.next: 季氏萌於釐公，大於成公，此其應也。

translations.chinese: 季氏萌於釐公，大於成公，此其應也。
translations.literal: Literal: The Ji clan began under Duke Xi and grew great under Duke Cheng; this was its response.
translations.idiomatic: The Ji clan began under Duke Xi and grew great under Duke Cheng; this was its response.

context.previous: 成公不寤，其冬季氏殺公子偃。
context.next: 董仲舒以為夷伯，季氏之孚也，陪臣不當有廟。

translations.chinese: 董仲舒以為夷伯，季氏之孚也，陪臣不當有廟。
translations.literal: Literal: Dong Zhongshu held Yi Bo was a dependent of the Ji clan; dependent ministers should not have their own temple.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held Yi Bo was a dependent of the Ji clan; dependent ministers should not have their own temple.

context.previous: 季氏萌於釐公，大於成公，此其應也。
context.next: 震者雷也，晦暝，雷擊其廟，明當絕去僭差之類也。

translations.chinese: 震者雷也，晦暝，雷擊其廟，明當絕去僭差之類也。
translations.literal: Literal: Thunder striking his temple in dark month-end showed that usurping, deviant kinds should be cut off.
translations.idiomatic: Thunder striking his temple in dark month-end showed that usurping, deviant kinds should be cut off.

context.previous: 董仲舒以為夷伯，季氏之孚也，陪臣不當有廟。
context.next: 向又以為此皆所謂夜妖者也。

translations.chinese: 向又以為此皆所謂夜妖者也。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang further held these were all so-called night anomalies.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang further held these were all so-called night anomalies.

context.previous: 震者雷也，晦暝，雷擊其廟，明當絕去僭差之類也。
context.next: 劉歆以為春秋及朔言朔，及晦言晦，人道所不及，則天震之。

translations.chinese: 劉歆以為春秋及朔言朔，及晦言晦，人道所不及，則天震之。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xin held that Spring and Autumn names the new moon as new moon and month-end as month-end; where human governance cannot reach, Heaven thunders it.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that Spring and Autumn names the new moon as new moon and month-end as month-end; where human governance cannot reach, Heaven thunders it.

context.previous: 向又以為此皆所謂夜妖者也。
context.next: 展氏有隱慝，故天加誅於其祖夷伯之廟以譴告之也。

translations.chinese: 展氏有隱慝，故天加誅於其祖夷伯之廟以譴告之也。
translations.literal: Literal: The Zhan clan had hidden wrongdoing, so Heaven punished their ancestor Yi Bo's temple to admonish them.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhan clan had hidden wrongdoing, so Heaven punished their ancestor Yi Bo's temple to admonish them.

context.previous: 劉歆以為春秋及朔言朔，及晦言晦，人道所不及，則天震之。

translations.chinese: 成公十六年「六月甲午晦，晉侯及楚子、鄭伯戰于鄢陵」。
translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Cheng year sixteen: "On jiawu day at month's end in sixth month, the marquis of Jin and the king of Chu and earl of Zheng fought at Yanling."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Cheng year sixteen: "On jiawu day at month's end in sixth month, the marquis of Jin and the king of Chu and earl of Zheng fought at Yanling."

context.next: 皆月晦云。

translations.chinese: 皆月晦云。
translations.literal: Literal: All happened at month's end.
translations.idiomatic: All happened at month's end.

context.previous: 成公十六年「六月甲午晦，晉侯及楚子、鄭伯戰于鄢陵」。

translations.chinese: 隱公五年「秋，螟」。
translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Yin year five: "In autumn, borers."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Yin year five: "In autumn, borers."

context.next: 董仲舒、劉向以為時公觀漁于棠，貪利之應也。

translations.chinese: 董仲舒、劉向以為時公觀漁于棠，貪利之應也。
translations.literal: Literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that the duke then watched fishing at Tang; this was a response to greed for profit.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that the duke then watched fishing at Tang; this was a response to greed for profit.

context.previous: 隱公五年「秋，螟」。
context.next: 劉歆以為又逆臧釐伯之諫，貪利區瓒，以生臝蟲之孽也。

translations.chinese: 劉歆以為又逆臧釐伯之諫，貪利區瓒，以生臝蟲之孽也。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xin held that he also rejected Zang Xibo's remonstrance and greedily coveted trifles, producing a prodigy of naked insects.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that he also rejected Zang Xibo's remonstrance and greedily coveted trifles, producing a prodigy of naked insects.

context.previous: 董仲舒、劉向以為時公觀漁于棠，貪利之應也。

translations.chinese: 八年「九月，螟」。
translations.literal: Literal: In year eight: "In the ninth month, borers."
translations.idiomatic: In year eight: "In the ninth month, borers."

context.next: 時鄭伯以邴將易許田，有貪利心。

translations.chinese: 時鄭伯以邴將易許田，有貪利心。
translations.literal: Literal: At that time the earl of Zheng sought to exchange Xu fields with Bing, showing greedy intent.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the earl of Zheng sought to exchange Xu fields with Bing, showing greedy intent.

context.previous: 八年「九月，螟」。
context.next: 京房易傳曰：「臣安祿茲謂貪，厥災蟲，蟲食根。

translations.chinese: 京房易傳曰：「臣安祿茲謂貪，厥災蟲，蟲食根。
translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When ministers rest secure in salary, this is called greed; its disaster is insects, and insects eat roots.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When ministers rest secure in salary, this is called greed; its disaster is insects, and insects eat roots.

context.previous: 時鄭伯以邴將易許田，有貪利心。
context.next: 德無常茲謂煩，蟲食葉。

translations.chinese: 德無常茲謂煩，蟲食葉。
translations.literal: Literal: When virtue lacks constancy, this is called vexation; insects eat leaves.
translations.idiomatic: When virtue lacks constancy, this is called vexation; insects eat leaves.

context.previous: 京房易傳曰：「臣安祿茲謂貪，厥災蟲，蟲食根。
context.next: 不絀無德，蟲食本。

translations.chinese: 不絀無德，蟲食本。
translations.literal: Literal: Not dismissing the unvirtuous, insects eat the trunk.
translations.idiomatic: Not dismissing the unvirtuous, insects eat the trunk.

context.previous: 德無常茲謂煩，蟲食葉。
context.next: 與東作爭，茲謂不時，蟲食節。

translations.chinese: 與東作爭，茲謂不時，蟲食節。
translations.literal: Literal: Competing with spring tilling is called untimely; insects eat nodes.
translations.idiomatic: Competing with spring tilling is called untimely; insects eat nodes.

context.previous: 不絀無德，蟲食本。
context.next: 蔽惡生孽，蟲食心。」

translations.chinese: 蔽惡生孽，蟲食心。」
translations.literal: Literal: Concealing evil breeds prodigies; insects eat the heart."
translations.idiomatic: Concealing evil breeds prodigies; insects eat the heart."

context.previous: 與東作爭，茲謂不時，蟲食節。

translations.chinese: 嚴公六年「秋，螟」。
translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Zhuang year six: "In autumn, borers."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhuang year six: "In autumn, borers."

context.next: 董仲舒、劉向以為先是衛侯朔出奔齊，齊侯會諸侯納朔，許諸侯賂。

translations.chinese: 董仲舒、劉向以為先是衛侯朔出奔齊，齊侯會諸侯納朔，許諸侯賂。
translations.literal: Literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that before this Duke Shuo of Wei fled to Qi; the marquis of Qi convened feudal lords to restore Shuo and accepted bribes from them.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that before this Duke Shuo of Wei fled to Qi; the marquis of Qi convened feudal lords to restore Shuo and accepted bribes from them.

context.previous: 嚴公六年「秋，螟」。
context.next: 齊人歸衛寶，魯受之，貪利應也。

translations.chinese: 齊人歸衛寶，魯受之，貪利應也。
translations.literal: Literal: Qi returned Wei treasures and Lu accepted them, a response to greed for profit.
translations.idiomatic: Qi returned Wei treasures and Lu accepted them, a response to greed for profit.

context.previous: 董仲舒、劉向以為先是衛侯朔出奔齊，齊侯會諸侯納朔，許諸侯賂。

translations.chinese: 文帝後六年秋，螟。
translations.literal: Literal: In autumn of Emperor Wen's later year six, borers appeared.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of Emperor Wen's later year six, borers appeared.

context.next: 是歲匈奴大入上郡、雲中，烽火通長安，遣三將軍屯邊，三將軍屯京師。

translations.chinese: 是歲匈奴大入上郡、雲中，烽火通長安，遣三將軍屯邊，三將軍屯京師。
translations.literal: Literal: That year the Xiongnu greatly invaded Shang and Yunzhong; beacon fires reached Chang'an, and three generals were sent to garrison the frontier and three to garrison the capital.
translations.idiomatic: That year the Xiongnu greatly invaded Shang and Yunzhong; beacon fires reached Chang'an, and three generals were sent to garrison the frontier and three to garrison the capital.

context.previous: 文帝後六年秋，螟。

translations.chinese: 宣公三年，「郊牛之口傷，改卜牛，牛死」。
translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Xuan year three: "The mouth of the suburban sacrificial ox was injured; divination was redone for another ox, and that ox died."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xuan year three: "The mouth of the suburban sacrificial ox was injured; divination was redone for another ox, and that ox died."

context.next: 劉向以為近牛禍也。

translations.chinese: 劉向以為近牛禍也。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang held this was a near ox calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held this was a near ox calamity.

context.previous: 宣公三年，「郊牛之口傷，改卜牛，牛死」。
context.next: 是時宣公與公子遂謀共殺子赤而立，又以喪娶，區霿昏亂。

translations.chinese: 是時宣公與公子遂謀共殺子赤而立，又以喪娶，區霿昏亂。
translations.literal: Literal: At that time Duke Xuan and Prince Sui plotted to kill Zichi and enthrone Xuan, and moreover married during mourning, obscured and chaotic.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Duke Xuan and Prince Sui plotted to kill Zichi and enthrone Xuan, and moreover married during mourning, obscured and chaotic.

context.previous: 劉向以為近牛禍也。
context.next: 亂成於口，幸有季文子得免於禍，天猶惡之，生則不饗其祀，死則災燔其廟。

translations.chinese: 亂成於口，幸有季文子得免於禍，天猶惡之，生則不饗其祀，死則災燔其廟。
translations.literal: Literal: Disorder was completed through the mouth. Luckily Jisun Wenzi escaped disaster; Heaven still hated it: alive, it did not accept his sacrifices; dead, disaster burned his temple.
translations.idiomatic: Disorder was completed through the mouth. Luckily Jisun Wenzi escaped disaster; Heaven still hated it: alive, it did not accept his sacrifices; dead, disaster burned his temple.

context.previous: 是時宣公與公子遂謀共殺子赤而立，又以喪娶，區霿昏亂。
context.next: 董仲舒指略同。

translations.chinese: 董仲舒指略同。
translations.literal: Literal: Dong Zhongshu's main point was roughly the same.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu's main point was roughly the same.

context.previous: 亂成於口，幸有季文子得免於禍，天猶惡之，生則不饗其祀，死則災燔其廟。

translations.chinese: 秦孝文王五年，斿朐衍，有獻五足牛者。
translations.literal: Literal: In year five of King Xiaowen of Qin, at Youquyan, someone presented a five-legged ox.
translations.idiomatic: In year five of King Xiaowen of Qin, at Youquyan, someone presented a five-legged ox.

context.next: 劉向以為近牛禍也。

translations.chinese: 劉向以為近牛禍也。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang held this was a near ox calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held this was a near ox calamity.

context.previous: 秦孝文王五年，斿朐衍，有獻五足牛者。
context.next: 先是文惠王初都咸陽，廣大宮室，南臨渭，北臨涇，思心失，逆土氣。

translations.idiomatic: Feet signify stopping; it warned Qin to halt extravagant grandeur, or it would bring danger and ruin.

translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took this as a near ox calamity.

translations.literal: Literal: He accepted the schemes of the wicked minister Yang Sheng, sought to become Han heir, and had deliberative minister Yuan Ang assassinated. When the matter was exposed, he returned bearing an axe and submitted to death.
translations.idiomatic: He accepted the schemes of the wicked minister Yang Sheng, sought to become Han heir, and had deliberative minister Yuan Ang assassinated. When the matter was exposed, he returned bearing an axe and submitted to death.

translations.idiomatic: After withdrawing to his state, he still harbored resentment: inwardly his thoughts were obscured and chaotic, outwardly his earthworks exceeded proper limits; therefore an ox calamity arose.

translations.idiomatic: A foot emerging from the back is the image of lower treachery against above.

translations.chinese: 夫天子省風以作樂，小者不窕，大者不摦。
translations.literal: Literal: The Son of Heaven examines winds to make music: small bells must not be too narrow, large bells must not be too broad.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven examines winds to make music: small bells must not be too narrow, large bells must not be too broad.

translations.chinese: 摦則不容，心是以感，感實生疾。

translations.idiomatic: If broad, they do not harmonize; the heart is affected by this, and feeling truly gives rise to illness.

context.next: 劉向以為是時景王好聽淫聲，適庶不明，思心霿亂，明年以心疾崩，近心腹之痾，凶短之極者也。

translations.chinese: 劉向以為是時景王好聽淫聲，適庶不明，思心霿亂，明年以心疾崩，近心腹之痾，凶短之極者也。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang held that at this time King Jing delighted in licentious sounds, failed to distinguish legitimate and secondary lines, and his thought-mind was obscured and chaotic; the next year he died of heart illness, a near affliction of heart and belly and extreme of violent shortening.

translations.chinese: 成帝建始元年四月辛丑夜，西北有如火光。

translations.idiomatic: When yang is submerged and cannot emerge, and yin is pressed and cannot rise, then there are earthquakes.

context.previous: 若過其序，民亂之也。
context.next: 今三川實震，是陽失其所而填陰也。

translations.chinese: 今三川實震，是陽失其所而填陰也。
translations.literal: Literal: Now the three rivers truly shook: this means yang lost its place and filled yin.
translations.idiomatic: Now the three rivers truly shook: this means yang lost its place and filled yin.

context.previous: 陽伏而不能出，陰迫而不能升，於是有地震。
context.next: 陽失而在陰，原必塞；

translations.chinese: 陽失而在陰，原必塞；
translations.literal: Literal: If yang is lost and lies in yin, the springs must be blocked;
translations.idiomatic: If yang is lost and lies in yin, the springs must be blocked;

context.previous: 今三川實震，是陽失其所而填陰也。
context.next: 原塞，國必亡。

translations.chinese: 原塞，國必亡。
translations.literal: Literal: if springs are blocked, the state must perish.
translations.idiomatic: if springs are blocked, the state must perish.

context.previous: 陽失而在陰，原必塞；
context.next: 夫水，土演而民用也；

translations.chinese: 夫水，土演而民用也；
translations.literal: Literal: Water is what earth channels, and people use it;
translations.idiomatic: Water is what earth channels, and people use it;

context.previous: 原塞，國必亡。
context.next: 土無所演，而民乏財用，不亡何待？

translations.chinese: 土無所演，而民乏財用，不亡何待？
translations.literal: Literal: if earth has nothing to channel, the people lack resources. If not perish then what awaits?
translations.idiomatic: if earth has nothing to channel, the people lack resources. If not perish then what awaits?

context.previous: 夫水，土演而民用也；
context.next: 昔伊雒竭而夏亡，河竭而商亡，今周德如二代之季，其原又塞，塞必竭；

translations.chinese: 昔伊雒竭而夏亡，河竭而商亡，今周德如二代之季，其原又塞，塞必竭；
translations.literal: Literal: Formerly when Yi and Luo dried up, Xia perished; when the Yellow dried up, Shang perished. Now Zhou's virtue is like the late generations of those two dynasties, and its springs are also blocked; blockage must lead to drying up.
translations.idiomatic: Formerly when Yi and Luo dried up, Xia perished; when the Yellow dried up, Shang perished. Now Zhou's virtue is like the late generations of those two dynasties, and its springs are also blocked; blockage must lead to drying up.

context.previous: 土無所演，而民乏財用，不亡何待？
context.next: 川竭，山必崩。

translations.chinese: 川竭，山必崩。
translations.literal: Literal: If rivers dry, mountains must collapse.
translations.idiomatic: If rivers dry, mountains must collapse.

context.previous: 昔伊雒竭而夏亡，河竭而商亡，今周德如二代之季，其原又塞，塞必竭；
context.next: 夫國必依山川，山崩川竭，亡之徵也。

translations.chinese: 夫國必依山川，山崩川竭，亡之徵也。
translations.literal: Literal: A state must rely on mountains and rivers; mountain collapse and river exhaustion are signs of ruin.
translations.idiomatic: A state must rely on mountains and rivers; mountain collapse and river exhaustion are signs of ruin.

context.previous: 川竭，山必崩。
context.next: 若國亡，不過十年，數之紀也。」

translations.chinese: 若國亡，不過十年，數之紀也。」
translations.literal: Literal: If the state perishes, it will be within ten years; this is the cycle of numbers."
translations.idiomatic: If the state perishes, it will be within ten years; this is the cycle of numbers."

context.previous: 夫國必依山川，山崩川竭，亡之徵也。

translations.chinese: 是歲二川竭，岐山崩。
translations.literal: Literal: That year two rivers dried up and Mount Qi collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: That year two rivers dried up and Mount Qi collapsed.

context.next: 劉向以為陽失在陰者，謂火氣來煎枯水，故川竭也。

translations.chinese: 劉向以為陽失在陰者，謂火氣來煎枯水，故川竭也。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang held that yang lost into yin means fire qi came and scorched water dry, hence river exhaustion.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that yang lost into yin means fire qi came and scorched water dry, hence river exhaustion.

context.previous: 是歲二川竭，岐山崩。
context.next: 山川連體，下竭上崩，事勢然也。

translations.chinese: 山川連體，下竭上崩，事勢然也。
translations.literal: Literal: Mountains and rivers are linked as one body: when below dries up, above collapses. Such is the force of things.
translations.idiomatic: Mountains and rivers are linked as one body: when below dries up, above collapses. Such is the force of things.

context.previous: 劉向以為陽失在陰者，謂火氣來煎枯水，故川竭也。
context.next: 時幽王暴虐，妄誅伐，不聽諫，迷於褒姒，廢其正后，廢后之父申侯與犬戎共攻殺幽王。

translations.chinese: 時幽王暴虐，妄誅伐，不聽諫，迷於褒姒，廢其正后，廢后之父申侯與犬戎共攻殺幽王。
translations.literal: Literal: At that time King You was violent and abusive, punished and attacked recklessly, would not heed remonstrance, was deluded by Bao Si, deposed his rightful queen; the deposed queen's father, Marquis of Shen, joined Quanrong to attack and kill King You.
translations.idiomatic: At that time King You was violent and abusive, punished and attacked recklessly, would not heed remonstrance, was deluded by Bao Si, deposed his rightful queen; the deposed queen's father, Marquis of Shen, joined Quanrong to attack and kill King You.

context.previous: 山川連體，下竭上崩，事勢然也。
context.next: 一曰，其在天文，水為辰星，辰星為蠻夷。

translations.chinese: 一曰，其在天文，水為辰星，辰星為蠻夷。
translations.literal: Literal: Another view: in astronomy, water is the planet Chenxing, and Chenxing represents barbarian peoples.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: in astronomy, water is the planet Chenxing, and Chenxing represents barbarian peoples.

context.previous: 時幽王暴虐，妄誅伐，不聽諫，迷於褒姒，廢其正后，廢后之父申侯與犬戎共攻殺幽王。
context.next: 月食辰星，國以女亡。

translations.chinese: 月食辰星，國以女亡。
translations.literal: Literal: When the moon eclipses Chenxing, a state perishes through women.
translations.idiomatic: When the moon eclipses Chenxing, a state perishes through women.

context.previous: 一曰，其在天文，水為辰星，辰星為蠻夷。
context.next: 幽王之敗，女亂其內，夷攻其外。

translations.chinese: 幽王之敗，女亂其內，夷攻其外。
translations.literal: Literal: In King You's defeat, women caused disorder within and barbarians attacked from without.
translations.idiomatic: In King You's defeat, women caused disorder within and barbarians attacked from without.

context.previous: 月食辰星，國以女亡。
context.next: 京房易傳曰：「君臣相背，厥異名水絕。」

translations.chinese: 京房易傳曰：「君臣相背，厥異名水絕。」
translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When ruler and ministers turn against each other, its anomaly is called waters cut off."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When ruler and ministers turn against each other, its anomaly is called waters cut off."

context.previous: 幽王之敗，女亂其內，夷攻其外。

translations.chinese: 文公九年「九月癸酉，地震」。
translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Wen year nine: "On guiyou day of the ninth month, earthquake."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Wen year nine: "On guiyou day of the ninth month, earthquake."

context.next: 劉向以為先是時，齊桓、晉文、魯釐二伯賢君新沒，周襄王失道，楚穆王殺父，諸侯皆不肖，權傾於下，天戒若曰，臣下彊盛者將動為害。

translations.chinese: 劉向以為先是時，齊桓、晉文、魯釐二伯賢君新沒，周襄王失道，楚穆王殺父，諸侯皆不肖，權傾於下，天戒若曰，臣下彊盛者將動為害。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang held that before this, the worthy hegemon-rulers Duke Huan of Qi, Duke Wen of Jin, and Duke Xi of Lu had newly died; King Xiang of Zhou had lost the Way; King Mu of Chu killed his father; the feudal lords were all unworthy and power tilted below. Heaven's warning was as if saying powerful ministers below would move to cause harm.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that before this, the worthy hegemon-rulers Duke Huan of Qi, Duke Wen of Jin, and Duke Xi of Lu had newly died; King Xiang of Zhou had lost the Way; King Mu of Chu killed his father; the feudal lords were all unworthy and power tilted below. Heaven's warning was as if saying powerful ministers below would move to cause harm.

context.previous: 文公九年「九月癸酉，地震」。
context.next: 後宋、魯、晉、莒、鄭、陳、齊皆殺君。

translations.chinese: 後宋、魯、晉、莒、鄭、陳、齊皆殺君。
translations.literal: Literal: Afterward Song, Lu, Jin, Ju, Zheng, Chen, and Qi all killed their rulers.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Song, Lu, Jin, Ju, Zheng, Chen, and Qi all killed their rulers.

context.previous: 劉向以為先是時，齊桓、晉文、魯釐二伯賢君新沒，周襄王失道，楚穆王殺父，諸侯皆不肖，權傾於下，天戒若曰，臣下彊盛者將動為害。
context.next: 諸震，略皆從董仲舒說也。

translations.chinese: 諸震，略皆從董仲舒說也。
translations.literal: Literal: Most earthquake cases generally follow Dong Zhongshu's explanation.
translations.idiomatic: Most earthquake cases generally follow Dong Zhongshu's explanation.

context.previous: 後宋、魯、晉、莒、鄭、陳、齊皆殺君。
context.next: 京房易傳曰：「臣事雖正，專必震，其震，於水則波，於木則搖，於屋則瓦落。

translations.chinese: 京房易傳曰：「臣事雖正，專必震，其震，於水則波，於木則搖，於屋則瓦落。
translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Even if ministerial service is upright, monopoly will still bring earthquake. In water it is waves, in wood shaking, in houses tiles falling.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Even if ministerial service is upright, monopoly will still bring earthquake. In water it is waves, in wood shaking, in houses tiles falling.

context.previous: 諸震，略皆從董仲舒說也。
context.next: 大經在辟而易臣，茲謂陰動，厥震搖政宮。

translations.chinese: 大經在辟而易臣，茲謂陰動，厥震搖政宮。
translations.literal: Literal: When the great norm lies with the ruler yet is shifted to ministers, this is called yin moving; its quake shakes government palaces.
translations.idiomatic: When the great norm lies with the ruler yet is shifted to ministers, this is called yin moving; its quake shakes government palaces.

context.previous: 京房易傳曰：「臣事雖正，專必震，其震，於水則波，於木則搖，於屋則瓦落。
context.next: 大經搖政，茲謂不陰，厥震搖山，山出涌水。

translations.chinese: 大經搖政，茲謂不陰，厥震搖山，山出涌水。
translations.literal: Literal: When the great norm of government is shaken, this is called non-yin; its quake shakes mountains, and surging waters emerge from mountains.
translations.idiomatic: When the great norm of government is shaken, this is called non-yin; its quake shakes mountains, and surging waters emerge from mountains.

context.previous: 大經在辟而易臣，茲謂陰動，厥震搖政宮。
context.next: 嗣子無德專祿，茲謂不順，厥震動丘陵，涌水出。」

translations.chinese: 嗣子無德專祿，茲謂不順，厥震動丘陵，涌水出。」
translations.literal: Literal: When an heir-son lacking virtue monopolizes emolument, this is called noncompliance; its quake moves hills and ridges and surging waters emerge."
translations.idiomatic: When an heir-son lacking virtue monopolizes emolument, this is called noncompliance; its quake moves hills and ridges and surging waters emerge."

context.previous: 大經搖政，茲謂不陰，厥震搖山，山出涌水。

translations.chinese: 襄公十六年「五月甲子，地震」。
translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Xiang year sixteen: "On jiazi day of the fifth month, earthquake."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xiang year sixteen: "On jiazi day of the fifth month, earthquake."

context.next: 劉向以為先是雞澤之會，諸侯盟，大夫又盟。

translations.chinese: 劉向以為先是雞澤之會，諸侯盟，大夫又盟。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang held that before this there was the assembly at Jize, where feudal lords covenanted and great officers also covenanted.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that before this there was the assembly at Jize, where feudal lords covenanted and great officers also covenanted.

context.previous: 襄公十六年「五月甲子，地震」。
context.next: 是歲三月，諸侯為溴梁之會，而大夫獨相與盟，五月地震矣。

translations.chinese: 是歲三月，諸侯為溴梁之會，而大夫獨相與盟，五月地震矣。
translations.literal: Literal: That year in third month, at the Chouliang assembly of feudal lords, the great officers alone covenanted with one another; by fifth month there was earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: That year in third month, at the Chouliang assembly of feudal lords, the great officers alone covenanted with one another; by fifth month there was earthquake.

context.previous: 劉向以為先是雞澤之會，諸侯盟，大夫又盟。
context.next: 其後崔氏專齊，欒盈亂晉，良霄傾鄭，閽殺吳子，燕逐其君，楚滅陳、蔡。

translations.chinese: 其後崔氏專齊，欒盈亂晉，良霄傾鄭，閽殺吳子，燕逐其君，楚滅陳、蔡。
translations.literal: Literal: Afterward the Cui clan monopolized Qi, Luan Ying threw Jin into disorder, Liang Xiao upended Zheng, gatekeepers killed the ruler of Wu, Yan expelled its ruler, and Chu destroyed Chen and Cai.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward the Cui clan monopolized Qi, Luan Ying threw Jin into disorder, Liang Xiao upended Zheng, gatekeepers killed the ruler of Wu, Yan expelled its ruler, and Chu destroyed Chen and Cai.

context.previous: 是歲三月，諸侯為溴梁之會，而大夫獨相與盟，五月地震矣。

translations.chinese: 昭公十九年「五月己卯，地震」。
translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Zhao year nineteen: "On jimao day of fifth month, earthquake."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhao year nineteen: "On jimao day of fifth month, earthquake."

context.next: 劉向以為是時季氏將有逐君之變。

translations.chinese: 劉向以為是時季氏將有逐君之變。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang held that at this time the Ji clan was about to produce the upheaval of expelling the ruler.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that at this time the Ji clan was about to produce the upheaval of expelling the ruler.

context.previous: 昭公十九年「五月己卯，地震」。
context.next: 其後宋三臣、曹會皆以地叛，蔡、莒逐其君，吳敗中國殺二君。

translations.chinese: 其後宋三臣、曹會皆以地叛，蔡、莒逐其君，吳敗中國殺二君。
translations.literal: Literal: Afterward three ministers in Song and Hui of Cao all rebelled with territory; Cai and Ju expelled their rulers; Wu defeated the central states and killed two rulers.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward three ministers in Song and Hui of Cao all rebelled with territory; Cai and Ju expelled their rulers; Wu defeated the central states and killed two rulers.

context.previous: 劉向以為是時季氏將有逐君之變。

translations.chinese: 二十三年「八月乙未，地震」。
translations.literal: Literal: In year twenty-three: "On yiwei day of eighth month, earthquake."
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-three: "On yiwei day of eighth month, earthquake."

context.next: 劉向以為是時周景王崩，劉、單立王子猛，尹氏立子朝。

translations.chinese: 劉向以為是時周景王崩，劉、單立王子猛，尹氏立子朝。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang held that at this time King Jing of Zhou died, the Liu and Shan line installed Prince Meng, and the Yin clan installed Prince Chao.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that at this time King Jing of Zhou died, the Liu and Shan line installed Prince Meng, and the Yin clan installed Prince Chao.

context.previous: 二十三年「八月乙未，地震」。
context.next: 其後季氏逐昭公，黑肱叛邾，吳殺其君僚，宋五大夫、晉二大夫皆以地叛。

translations.chinese: 其後季氏逐昭公，黑肱叛邾，吳殺其君僚，宋五大夫、晉二大夫皆以地叛。
translations.literal: Literal: Afterward the Ji clan expelled Duke Zhao; Heigong rebelled in Zhu; Wu killed its ruler Liao; five grandees of Song and two grandees of Jin all rebelled with territory.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward the Ji clan expelled Duke Zhao; Heigong rebelled in Zhu; Wu killed its ruler Liao; five grandees of Song and two grandees of Jin all rebelled with territory.

context.previous: 劉向以為是時周景王崩，劉、單立王子猛，尹氏立子朝。

translations.chinese: 哀公三年「四月甲午，地震」。
translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Ai year three: "On jiawu day of fourth month, earthquake."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Ai year three: "On jiawu day of fourth month, earthquake."

context.next: 劉向以為是時諸侯皆信邪臣，莫能用仲尼，盜殺蔡侯，齊陳乞弒君。

translations.chinese: 劉向以為是時諸侯皆信邪臣，莫能用仲尼，盜殺蔡侯，齊陳乞弒君。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang held that at this time all feudal lords trusted wicked ministers and none could employ Confucius; the marquis of Cai was assassinated, and Chen Qi of Qi regicide-killed his ruler.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that at this time all feudal lords trusted wicked ministers and none could employ Confucius; the marquis of Cai was assassinated, and Chen Qi of Qi regicide-killed his ruler.

context.previous: 哀公三年「四月甲午，地震」。

translations.chinese: 惠帝二年正月，地震隴西，厭四百餘家。
translations.literal: Literal: In first month of Emperor Hui year two, earthquake struck Longxi, burying over four hundred households.
translations.idiomatic: In first month of Emperor Hui year two, earthquake struck Longxi, burying over four hundred households.

context.next: 武帝征和二年八月癸亥，地震，厭殺人。

translations.chinese: 武帝征和二年八月癸亥，地震，厭殺人。
translations.literal: Literal: On guihai day of eighth month, Zhenghe year two under Emperor Wu, earthquake struck and crushed people to death.
translations.idiomatic: On guihai day of eighth month, Zhenghe year two under Emperor Wu, earthquake struck and crushed people to death.

context.previous: 惠帝二年正月，地震隴西，厭四百餘家。
context.next: 宣帝本始四年四月壬寅，地震河南以東四十九郡，北海琅邪壞祖宗廟城郭，殺六千餘人。

translations.chinese: 宣帝本始四年四月壬寅，地震河南以東四十九郡，北海琅邪壞祖宗廟城郭，殺六千餘人。
translations.literal: Literal: On renyin day of fourth month, Benshi year four under Emperor Xuan, earthquake struck forty-nine commanderies east of Henan; in Beihai and Langya ancestral temples and city walls collapsed, killing over six thousand people.
translations.idiomatic: On renyin day of fourth month, Benshi year four under Emperor Xuan, earthquake struck forty-nine commanderies east of Henan; in Beihai and Langya ancestral temples and city walls collapsed, killing over six thousand people.

context.previous: 武帝征和二年八月癸亥，地震，厭殺人。
context.next: 元帝永光三年冬，地震。

translations.chinese: 元帝永光三年冬，地震。
translations.literal: Literal: In winter of Yongguang year three under Emperor Yuan, earthquake occurred.
translations.idiomatic: In winter of Yongguang year three under Emperor Yuan, earthquake occurred.

context.previous: 宣帝本始四年四月壬寅，地震河南以東四十九郡，北海琅邪壞祖宗廟城郭，殺六千餘人。
context.next: 綏和二年九月丙辰，地震，自京師至北邊郡國三十餘壞城郭，凡殺四百一十五人。

translations.chinese: 綏和二年九月丙辰，地震，自京師至北邊郡國三十餘壞城郭，凡殺四百一十五人。
translations.literal: Literal: On bingchen day of ninth month, Suihe year two, earthquake from the capital to northern border commanderies and kingdoms damaged over thirty city walls, killing 415 people in total.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen day of ninth month, Suihe year two, earthquake from the capital to northern border commanderies and kingdoms damaged over thirty city walls, killing 415 people in total.

context.previous: 元帝永光三年冬，地震。

translations.chinese: 釐公十四年「秋八月辛卯，沙麓崩」。
translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Xi year fourteen: "On xinmao day of the eighth month in autumn, Shalu collapsed."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi year fourteen: "On xinmao day of the eighth month in autumn, Shalu collapsed."

context.next: 穀梁傳曰：「林屬於山曰麓，沙其名也。」

translations.chinese: 穀梁傳曰：「林屬於山曰麓，沙其名也。」
translations.literal: Literal: The Guliang Tradition says: "The woods attached to a mountain are called lu; Sha is its name."
translations.idiomatic: The Guliang Tradition says: "The woods attached to a mountain are called lu; Sha is its name."

context.previous: 釐公十四年「秋八月辛卯，沙麓崩」。
context.next: 劉向以為臣下背叛，散落不事上之象也。

translations.chinese: 劉向以為臣下背叛，散落不事上之象也。

translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held this symbolized ministers below betraying and scattering, not serving above.

translations.chinese: 凡君道傷者病天氣，不言五行沴天，而曰「日月亂行，星辰逆行」者，為若下不敢沴天，猶春秋曰「王師敗績于貿戎」，不言敗之者，以自敗為文，尊尊之意也。
translations.literal: Literal: Whenever rulerly way is harmed, heavenly qi is diseased. It does not say five phases afflict Heaven, but says sun and moon move disorderly and stars in reverse, because inferiors dare not be said to afflict Heaven, just as Spring and Autumn says "the royal army was defeated at Mao-Rong" without naming who defeated it, expressing reverence for the honored.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever rulerly way is harmed, heavenly qi is diseased. It does not say five phases afflict Heaven, but says sun and moon move disorderly and stars in reverse, because inferiors dare not be said to afflict Heaven, just as Spring and Autumn says "the royal army was defeated at Mao-Rong" without naming who defeated it, expressing reverence for the honored.

translations.idiomatic: His explanation says inferiors attacking superiors means Heaven's punishment already complete, so it should not again be called an affliction.

translations.literal: Literal: In Duke Zhuang year eighteen: "In autumn, there were yu insects."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhuang year eighteen: "In autumn, there were yu insects."

context.next: 劉向以為蜮生南越。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang held yu were born in Nanyue.

translations.idiomatic: In Yue lands there are many women; men and women share rivers, with licentious women dominant. From disordered qi this creature is born; therefore sages named it yu.

translations.idiomatic: Yu is like confusion: by watersides it can shoot people, and where it shoots there are sores, severe cases reaching death.

translations.idiomatic: At that time among the central states Qi and Jin were strong, and among southern tribes Wu and Chu were strong. Chen, between them, was not close to Jin nor firm with Chu, and repeatedly suffered the two states' calamities.

translations.literal: Literal: After they had gone, they saw the abandoned uncanny child of that palace woman, heard its crying at night, pitied it, took it in, and fled to Bao.
translations.idiomatic: After they had gone, they saw the abandoned uncanny child of that palace woman, heard its crying at night, pitied it, took it in, and fled to Bao.

context.previous: 後有夫婦鬻是器者，宣王使執而僇之。

translations.chinese: 後褒人有罪，入妖子以贖，是為褒姒，幽王見而愛之，生子伯服。
translations.literal: Literal: Later, when Bao people had committed offense, they presented the uncanny child as ransom. This became Bao Si; King You saw and loved her, and she bore Bo Fu.
translations.idiomatic: Later, when Bao people had committed offense, they presented the uncanny child as ransom. This became Bao Si; King You saw and loved her, and she bore Bo Fu.

translations.chinese: 王廢申后及太子宜咎，而立褒姒、伯服代之。
translations.literal: Literal: The king deposed Empress Shen and crown prince Yijiu, and established Bao Si and Bo Fu in their place.
translations.idiomatic: The king deposed Empress Shen and crown prince Yijiu, and established Bao Si and Bo Fu in their place.

translations.chinese: 廢后之父申侯與繒西畎戎共攻殺幽王。
translations.literal: Literal: The deposed empress's father, Marquis of Shen, with Zeng and the western Quanrong, jointly attacked and killed King You.
translations.idiomatic: The deposed empress's father, Marquis of Shen, with Zeng and the western Quanrong, jointly attacked and killed King You.

context.previous: 王廢申后及太子宜咎，而立褒姒、伯服代之。

translations.chinese: 《詩》曰：「赫赫宗周，褒姒醤之。」
translations.literal: Literal: The Odes says: "Brilliant ancestral Zhou, Bao Si destroyed it."
translations.idiomatic: The Odes says: "Brilliant ancestral Zhou, Bao Si destroyed it."

context.previous: 廢后之父申侯與繒西畎戎共攻殺幽王。

translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang held that in late Xia and in Zhou under You and Li, all were perverse and against Heaven; therefore there were the strange dragon-lizard events, near prodigies of dragons and snakes.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that in late Xia and in Zhou under You and Li, all were perverse and against Heaven; therefore there were the strange dragon-lizard events, near prodigies of dragons and snakes.

translations.literal: Literal: Spittle means blood; another view says foam.
translations.idiomatic: Spittle means blood; another view says foam.

context.previous: 劉向以為夏后季世，周之幽、厲，皆誖亂逆天，故有龍黿之怪，近龍蛇孽也。

translations.chinese: 览弧，桑弓也。

translations.idiomatic: Lan-bow means mulberry bow.

translations.idiomatic: Qi-quiver likely means arrow quiver made from qi grass, near an archery anomaly.

context.previous: 览弧，桑弓也。

translations.chinese: 女童謠者，禍將生於女，國以兵寇亡也。
translations.literal: Literal: The girls' rhyme meant disaster would be born from a woman, and the state would perish through war and banditry.
translations.idiomatic: The girls' rhyme meant disaster would be born from a woman, and the state would perish through war and banditry.

translations.chinese: 左氏傳昭公十九年，龍鬥於鄭時門之外洧淵。
translations.literal: Literal: The Zuo Tradition says in Duke Zhao year nineteen, dragons fought outside Shi Gate of Zheng at the Wei abyss.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says in Duke Zhao year nineteen, dragons fought outside Shi Gate of Zheng at the Wei abyss.

context.next: 劉向以為近龍孽也。

translations.chinese: 劉向以為近龍孽也。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang took this as a near dragon prodigy.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took this as a near dragon prodigy.

context.previous: 左氏傳昭公十九年，龍鬥於鄭時門之外洧淵。
context.next: 鄭以小國攝乎晉楚之間，重以彊吳，鄭當其衝，不能修德，將鬥三國，以自危亡。

translations.chinese: 鄭以小國攝乎晉楚之間，重以彊吳，鄭當其衝，不能修德，將鬥三國，以自危亡。
translations.literal: Literal: Zheng, a small state caught between Jin and Chu and also strong Wu, stood at the collision point. Unable to cultivate virtue, it would contend among three states and endanger itself.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng, a small state caught between Jin and Chu and also strong Wu, stood at the collision point. Unable to cultivate virtue, it would contend among three states and endanger itself.

context.previous: 劉向以為近龍孽也。
context.next: 是時子產任政，內惠於民，外善辭令，以交三國，鄭卒亡患，能以德消變之效也。

translations.chinese: 是時子產任政，內惠於民，外善辭令，以交三國，鄭卒亡患，能以德消變之效也。
translations.literal: Literal: At that time Zichan governed affairs, beneficent to the people within and skillful in diplomacy without, engaging all three states; Zheng ultimately escaped disaster, effect of dispelling change through virtue.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Zichan governed affairs, beneficent to the people within and skillful in diplomacy without, engaging all three states; Zheng ultimately escaped disaster, effect of dispelling change through virtue.

context.previous: 鄭以小國攝乎晉楚之間，重以彊吳，鄭當其衝，不能修德，將鬥三國，以自危亡。
context.next: 京房易傳曰：「眾心不安，厥妖龍鬥。」

translations.chinese: 京房易傳曰：「眾心不安，厥妖龍鬥。」
translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When people's hearts are unsettled, its portent is dragons fighting."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When people's hearts are unsettled, its portent is dragons fighting."

context.previous: 是時子產任政，內惠於民，外善辭令，以交三國，鄭卒亡患，能以德消變之效也。

translations.chinese: 惠帝二年正月癸酉旦，有兩龍見於蘭陵廷東里溫陵井中，至乙亥夜去。
translations.literal: Literal: At dawn on guiyou day of first month, Emperor Hui year two, two dragons appeared in Wenling Well in East Lane of Lanling court, and departed on the night of yihai.
translations.idiomatic: At dawn on guiyou day of first month, Emperor Hui year two, two dragons appeared in Wenling Well in East Lane of Lanling court, and departed on the night of yihai.

context.next: 劉向以為龍貴象而困於庶人井中，象諸侯將有幽執之禍。

translations.chinese: 劉向以為龍貴象而困於庶人井中，象諸侯將有幽執之禍。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang held that dragons, symbols of nobility, trapped in a commoner's well symbolized feudal lords suffering imprisonment and death.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that dragons, symbols of nobility, trapped in a commoner's well symbolized feudal lords suffering imprisonment and death.

context.previous: 惠帝二年正月癸酉旦，有兩龍見於蘭陵廷東里溫陵井中，至乙亥夜去。
context.next: 其後呂太后幽殺三趙王，諸呂亦終誅滅。

translations.chinese: 其後呂太后幽殺三趙王，諸呂亦終誅滅。
translations.literal: Literal: Afterward Empress Dowager Lü imprisoned and killed three kings of Zhao, and the Lü clans were ultimately all exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Empress Dowager Lü imprisoned and killed three kings of Zhao, and the Lü clans were ultimately all exterminated.

context.previous: 劉向以為龍貴象而困於庶人井中，象諸侯將有幽執之禍。
context.next: 京房易傳曰：「有德遭害，厥妖龍見井中。」

translations.chinese: 京房易傳曰：「有德遭害，厥妖龍見井中。」
translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the virtuous suffer harm, its portent is dragons appearing in a well."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the virtuous suffer harm, its portent is dragons appearing in a well."

context.previous: 其後呂太后幽殺三趙王，諸呂亦終誅滅。
context.next: 又曰：「行刑暴惡，黑龍從井出。」

translations.chinese: 又曰：「行刑暴惡，黑龍從井出。」
translations.literal: Literal: It also says: "When punishments are violent and evil, black dragons emerge from wells."
translations.idiomatic: It also says: "When punishments are violent and evil, black dragons emerge from wells."

context.previous: 京房易傳曰：「有德遭害，厥妖龍見井中。」

translations.chinese: 左氏傳魯嚴公時有內蛇與外蛇鬥鄭南門中，內蛇死。
translations.literal: Literal: The Zuo Tradition says in Duke Zhuang of Lu's time, an inner snake and outer snake fought in Zheng's South Gate, and the inner snake died.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says in Duke Zhuang of Lu's time, an inner snake and outer snake fought in Zheng's South Gate, and the inner snake died.

context.next: 劉向以為近蛇孽也。

translations.chinese: 劉向以為近蛇孽也。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang took this as a near snake prodigy.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took this as a near snake prodigy.

context.previous: 左氏傳魯嚴公時有內蛇與外蛇鬥鄭南門中，內蛇死。
context.next: 先是鄭厲公劫相祭仲而逐兄昭公代立。

translations.chinese: 先是鄭厲公劫相祭仲而逐兄昭公代立。
translations.literal: Literal: Before this, Duke Li of Zheng coerced his minister Ji Zhong and expelled his elder brother Duke Zhao to take the throne.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, Duke Li of Zheng coerced his minister Ji Zhong and expelled his elder brother Duke Zhao to take the throne.

context.previous: 劉向以為近蛇孽也。
context.next: 後厲公出奔，昭公復入。

translations.chinese: 後厲公出奔，昭公復入。
translations.literal: Literal: Later Duke Li fled into exile and Duke Zhao returned.
translations.idiomatic: Later Duke Li fled into exile and Duke Zhao returned.

context.previous: 先是鄭厲公劫相祭仲而逐兄昭公代立。
context.next: 死，弟子儀代立。

translations.chinese: 死，弟子儀代立。
translations.literal: Literal: After Zhao died, his younger brother Ziyi succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: After Zhao died, his younger brother Ziyi succeeded.

context.previous: 後厲公出奔，昭公復入。
context.next: 厲公自外劫大夫傅瑕，使僇子儀。

translations.chinese: 厲公自外劫大夫傅瑕，使僇子儀。
translations.literal: Literal: From abroad Duke Li coerced grandee Fu Xia to execute Ziyi.
translations.idiomatic: From abroad Duke Li coerced grandee Fu Xia to execute Ziyi.

context.previous: 死，弟子儀代立。
context.next: 此外蛇殺內蛇之象也。

translations.chinese: 此外蛇殺內蛇之象也。
translations.literal: Literal: This was the image of outer snake killing inner snake.
translations.idiomatic: This was the image of outer snake killing inner snake.

context.previous: 厲公自外劫大夫傅瑕，使僇子儀。
context.next: 蛇死六年，而厲公立。

translations.chinese: 蛇死六年，而厲公立。
translations.literal: Literal: Six years after the snake's death, Duke Li was established.
translations.idiomatic: Six years after the snake's death, Duke Li was established.

context.previous: 此外蛇殺內蛇之象也。
context.next: 嚴公聞之，問申繻曰：「猶有妖乎？」

translations.chinese: 嚴公聞之，問申繻曰：「猶有妖乎？」
translations.literal: Literal: Duke Zhuang heard this and asked Shen Xu: "Are there still portents?"
translations.idiomatic: Duke Zhuang heard this and asked Shen Xu: "Are there still portents?"

context.previous: 蛇死六年，而厲公立。
context.next: 對曰：「人之所忌，其氣炎以取之，妖由人興也。

translations.chinese: 對曰：「人之所忌，其氣炎以取之，妖由人興也。
translations.literal: Literal: He replied: "What people dread, their qi flares to seize; anomalies rise from people.
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "What people dread, their qi flares to seize; anomalies rise from people.

context.previous: 嚴公聞之，問申繻曰：「猶有妖乎？」
context.next: 人亡舋焉，妖不自作。

translations.chinese: 人亡舋焉，妖不自作。
translations.literal: Literal: If people have no crack for them, anomalies do not make themselves.
translations.idiomatic: If people have no crack for them, anomalies do not make themselves.

context.previous: 對曰：「人之所忌，其氣炎以取之，妖由人興也。
context.next: 人棄常，故有妖。」

translations.chinese: 人棄常，故有妖。」
translations.literal: Literal: When people abandon constancy, therefore anomalies occur."
translations.idiomatic: When people abandon constancy, therefore anomalies occur."

context.previous: 人亡舋焉，妖不自作。
context.next: 京房易傳曰：「立嗣子疑，厥妖蛇居國門鬥。」

translations.chinese: 京房易傳曰：「立嗣子疑，厥妖蛇居國門鬥。」
translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the heir is set up in doubt, its portent is snakes fighting at the state gate."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the heir is set up in doubt, its portent is snakes fighting at the state gate."

context.previous: 人棄常，故有妖。」

translations.chinese: 武帝太始四年七月，趙有蛇從郭外入，與邑中蛇鬥孝文廟下，邑中蛇死。
translations.literal: Literal: In seventh month of Taishi year four under Emperor Wu, in Zhao a snake came in from outside city walls and fought snakes inside the district below the Temple of Emperor Xiaowen; the inner snakes died.
translations.idiomatic: In seventh month of Taishi year four under Emperor Wu, in Zhao a snake came in from outside city walls and fought snakes inside the district below the Temple of Emperor Xiaowen; the inner snakes died.

context.next: 後二年秋，有衛太子事，事自趙人江充起。

translations.chinese: 後二年秋，有衛太子事，事自趙人江充起。
translations.literal: Literal: Two years later, in autumn, there was the affair of the Crown Prince Wei, and the affair began from Jiang Chong of Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Two years later, in autumn, there was the affair of the Crown Prince Wei, and the affair began from Jiang Chong of Zhao.

context.previous: 武帝太始四年七月，趙有蛇從郭外入，與邑中蛇鬥孝文廟下，邑中蛇死。

translations.chinese: 左氏傳定公十年，宋公子地有白馬駟，公嬖向魋欲之，公取而朱其尾鬣以予之。
translations.literal: Literal: The Zuo Tradition says in Duke Ding year ten: Prince Di of Song had a team of four white horses. The duke favored Xiang Tui and wanted them; the duke took them, painted their tails and manes red, and gave them to Xiang Tui.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says in Duke Ding year ten: Prince Di of Song had a team of four white horses. The duke favored Xiang Tui and wanted them; the duke took them, painted their tails and manes red, and gave them to Xiang Tui.

context.next: 地怒，使其徒抶魋而奪之。

translations.chinese: 地怒，使其徒抶魋而奪之。
translations.literal: Literal: Di, enraged, had his followers beat Xiang Tui and seize them back.
translations.idiomatic: Di, enraged, had his followers beat Xiang Tui and seize them back.

context.previous: 左氏傳定公十年，宋公子地有白馬駟，公嬖向魋欲之，公取而朱其尾鬣以予之。
context.next: 魋懼將走，公閉門而泣之，目盡腫。

translations.chinese: 魋懼將走，公閉門而泣之，目盡腫。
translations.literal: Literal: Xiang Tui, fearful, was about to flee; the duke shut the gate and wept for him until his eyes were swollen.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Tui, fearful, was about to flee; the duke shut the gate and wept for him until his eyes were swollen.

context.previous: 地怒，使其徒抶魋而奪之。
context.next: 公弟辰謂地曰：「子為君禮，不過出竟，君必止子。」

translations.chinese: 公弟辰謂地曰：「子為君禮，不過出竟，君必止子。」
translations.literal: Literal: The duke's younger brother Chen told Di: "If you observe ruler-minister propriety, do no more than leave the border; the ruler will surely stop you."
translations.idiomatic: The duke's younger brother Chen told Di: "If you observe ruler-minister propriety, do no more than leave the border; the ruler will surely stop you."

context.previous: 魋懼將走，公閉門而泣之，目盡腫。
context.next: 地出奔陳，公弗止。

translations.chinese: 地出奔陳，公弗止。
translations.literal: Literal: Di fled to Chen; the duke did not stop him.
translations.idiomatic: Di fled to Chen; the duke did not stop him.

context.previous: 公弟辰謂地曰：「子為君禮，不過出竟，君必止子。」
context.next: 辰為之請，不聽。

translations.chinese: 辰為之請，不聽。
translations.literal: Literal: Chen pleaded for him and was not heeded.
translations.idiomatic: Chen pleaded for him and was not heeded.

context.previous: 地出奔陳，公弗止。
context.next: 辰曰：「是我迋吾兄也，吾以國人出，君誰與處？」

translations.chinese: 辰曰：「是我迋吾兄也，吾以國人出，君誰與處？」
translations.literal: Literal: Chen said: "Then I have deceived my elder brother. If I leave with the people of the state, with whom will the ruler remain?"
translations.idiomatic: Chen said: "Then I have deceived my elder brother. If I leave with the people of the state, with whom will the ruler remain?"

context.previous: 辰為之請，不聽。
context.next: 遂與其徒出奔陳。

translations.chinese: 遂與其徒出奔陳。
translations.literal: Literal: So he and his followers also fled to Chen.
translations.idiomatic: So he and his followers also fled to Chen.

context.previous: 辰曰：「是我迋吾兄也，吾以國人出，君誰與處？」
context.next: 明年俱入于蕭以叛，大為宋患，近馬禍也。

translations.chinese: 明年俱入于蕭以叛，大為宋患，近馬禍也。
translations.literal: Literal: The next year both entered Xiao and rebelled, causing great trouble for Song, a near horse calamity.
translations.idiomatic: The next year both entered Xiao and rebelled, causing great trouble for Song, a near horse calamity.

context.previous: 遂與其徒出奔陳。

translations.chinese: 史記秦孝公二十一年有馬生人，昭王二十年牡馬生子而死。
translations.literal: Literal: The Records says: in year twenty-one of King Xiaogong of Qin, there was a horse giving birth to a human; in year twenty of King Zhao, a stallion gave birth and then died.
translations.idiomatic: The Records says: in year twenty-one of King Xiaogong of Qin, there was a horse giving birth to a human; in year twenty of King Zhao, a stallion gave birth and then died.

context.next: 劉向以為皆馬禍也。

translations.chinese: 劉向以為皆馬禍也。
translations.literal: Literal: Liu Xiang took both as horse calamities.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took both as horse calamities.

context.previous: 史記秦孝公二十一年有馬生人，昭王二十年牡馬生子而死。
context.next: 孝公始用商君攻守之法，東侵諸侯，至於昭王，用兵彌烈。

translations.chinese: 孝公始用商君攻守之法，東侵諸侯，至於昭王，用兵彌烈。
translations.literal: Literal: Xiaogong first adopted Lord Shang's offensive-defensive laws and invaded feudal lords eastward; by King Zhao, warfare was even fiercer.
translations.idiomatic: Xiaogong first adopted Lord Shang's offensive-defensive laws and invaded feudal lords eastward; by King Zhao, warfare was even fiercer.

context.previous: 劉向以為皆馬禍也。
context.next: 其象將以兵革抗極成功，而還自害也。

translations.chinese: 其象將以兵革抗極成功，而還自害也。
translations.literal: Literal: Its image was that they would rely on military force to achieve extreme success, then harm themselves in return.
translations.idiomatic: Its image was that they would rely on military force to achieve extreme success, then harm themselves in return.

context.previous: 孝公始用商君攻守之法，東侵諸侯，至於昭王，用兵彌烈。
context.next: 牡馬非生類，妄生而死，猶秦恃力彊得天下，而還自滅之象也。

translations.chinese: 牡馬非生類，妄生而死，猶秦恃力彊得天下，而還自滅之象也。
