translations.chinese: 哀帝建平中，豫章有男子化為女子，嫁為人婦，生一子。
translations.literal: Literal: During Jianping under Emperor Ai, in Yuzhang there was a man transformed into a woman, married as a wife, and bore one son.
translations.idiomatic: During Jianping under Emperor Ai, in Yuzhang there was a man transformed into a woman, married as a wife, and bore one son.

context.next: 長安陳鳳言此陽變為陰，將亡繼嗣，自相生之象。

translations.chinese: 長安陳鳳言此陽變為陰，將亡繼嗣，自相生之象。
translations.literal: Literal: Chen Feng of Chang'an said this was yang transforming into yin, meaning heir succession would end, an image of self-generation.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Feng of Chang'an said this was yang transforming into yin, meaning heir succession would end, an image of self-generation.

context.previous: 哀帝建平中，豫章有男子化為女子，嫁為人婦，生一子。
context.next: 一曰，嫁為人婦生一子，將復一世乃絕。

translations.chinese: 一曰，嫁為人婦生一子，將復一世乃絕。
translations.literal: Literal: Another view: marrying as wife and bearing one son means one more generation then extinction.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: marrying as wife and bearing one son means one more generation then extinction.

context.previous: 長安陳鳳言此陽變為陰，將亡繼嗣，自相生之象。

translations.chinese: 哀帝建平四年四月，山陽方與女子田無嗇生子。
translations.literal: Literal: In fourth month of Jianping year four under Emperor Ai, in Shan'yang, a woman Fangyu and daughter Tian Wuse gave birth to a son.
translations.idiomatic: In fourth month of Jianping year four under Emperor Ai, in Shan'yang, a woman Fangyu and daughter Tian Wuse gave birth to a son.

context.next: 先未生二月，兒啼腹中，及生，不舉，葬之陌上，三日，人過聞啼聲，母掘收養。

translations.chinese: 先未生二月，兒啼腹中，及生，不舉，葬之陌上，三日，人過聞啼聲，母掘收養。
translations.literal: Literal: Two months before birth the child cried in the womb; when born it was not raised and was buried by the roadside. After three days passersby heard crying, and the mother dug it up and raised it.
translations.idiomatic: Two months before birth the child cried in the womb; when born it was not raised and was buried by the roadside. After three days passersby heard crying, and the mother dug it up and raised it.

context.previous: 哀帝建平四年四月，山陽方與女子田無嗇生子。

translations.chinese: 平帝元始元年二月，朔方廣牧女子趙春病死，斂棺積六日，出在棺外，自言見夫死父，曰：「年二十七，不當死。」
translations.literal: Literal: In second month of Yuanshi year one under Emperor Ping, in Guangmu, Shuofang, a woman Zhao Chun fell ill and died. After six days sealed in coffin, she came out outside the coffin and said she had seen her dead husband and father, saying, "At age twenty-seven, I should not die."
translations.idiomatic: In second month of Yuanshi year one under Emperor Ping, in Guangmu, Shuofang, a woman Zhao Chun fell ill and died. After six days sealed in coffin, she came out outside the coffin and said she had seen her dead husband and father, saying, "At age twenty-seven, I should not die."

context.next: 太守譚以聞。

translations.chinese: 太守譚以聞。
translations.literal: Literal: Governor Tan reported it.
translations.idiomatic: Governor Tan reported it.

context.previous: 平帝元始元年二月，朔方廣牧女子趙春病死，斂棺積六日，出在棺外，自言見夫死父，曰：「年二十七，不當死。」
context.next: 京房易傳曰：「『幹父之蠱，有子，考亡咎』。

translations.chinese: 京房易傳曰：「『幹父之蠱，有子，考亡咎』。
translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: ""Handling father's corruption: there is a son; the deceased father has no blame."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: ""Handling father's corruption: there is a son; the deceased father has no blame."

context.previous: 太守譚以聞。
context.next: 子三年不改父道，思慕不皇，亦重見先人之非，不則為私，厥妖人死復生。」

translations.chinese: 子三年不改父道，思慕不皇，亦重見先人之非，不則為私，厥妖人死復生。」
translations.literal: Literal: If for three years the son does not alter the father's way and, in longing thought, is not at ease, he may again see forebears' wrongs; otherwise it turns private. Its portent is the dead returning to life."
translations.idiomatic: If for three years the son does not alter the father's way and, in longing thought, is not at ease, he may again see forebears' wrongs; otherwise it turns private. Its portent is the dead returning to life."

context.previous: 京房易傳曰：「『幹父之蠱，有子，考亡咎』。
context.next: 一曰，至陰為陽，下人為上。

translations.chinese: 一曰，至陰為陽，下人為上。
translations.literal: Literal: Another view: utmost yin becoming yang, inferiors becoming superiors.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: utmost yin becoming yang, inferiors becoming superiors.

context.previous: 子三年不改父道，思慕不皇，亦重見先人之非，不則為私，厥妖人死復生。」

translations.chinese: 六月，長安女子有生兒，兩頭異頸面相鄉，四臂共匈俱前鄉，犊上有目長二寸所。
translations.literal: Literal: In sixth month, a woman in Chang'an gave birth to a child with two heads on different necks facing each other, four arms sharing one chest and facing forward together, and an eye on the lower body about two cun long.
translations.idiomatic: In sixth month, a woman in Chang'an gave birth to a child with two heads on different necks facing each other, four arms sharing one chest and facing forward together, and an eye on the lower body about two cun long.

context.next: 京房易傳曰：「『睽孤，見豕負塗』，厥妖人生兩頭。

translations.chinese: 京房易傳曰：「『睽孤，見豕負塗』，厥妖人生兩頭。
translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: ""Isolated in opposition, one sees swine covered in mud"; its portent is humans born with two heads.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: ""Isolated in opposition, one sees swine covered in mud"; its portent is humans born with two heads.

context.previous: 六月，長安女子有生兒，兩頭異頸面相鄉，四臂共匈俱前鄉，犊上有目長二寸所。
context.next: 下相攘善，妖亦同。

translations.chinese: 下相攘善，妖亦同。
translations.literal: Literal: When those below contend over good, the portent is likewise so.
translations.idiomatic: When those below contend over good, the portent is likewise so.

context.previous: 京房易傳曰：「『睽孤，見豕負塗』，厥妖人生兩頭。
context.next: 人若六畜首目在下，茲謂亡上，正將變更。

translations.chinese: 人若六畜首目在下，茲謂亡上，正將變更。
translations.literal: Literal: If humans or livestock have head and eyes below, this is called no superior, and proper order will change.
translations.idiomatic: If humans or livestock have head and eyes below, this is called no superior, and proper order will change.

context.previous: 下相攘善，妖亦同。
context.next: 凡妖之作，以譴失正，各象其類。

translations.chinese: 凡妖之作，以譴失正，各象其類。
translations.literal: Literal: Whenever portents arise, they admonish loss of correctness, each resembling its own class.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever portents arise, they admonish loss of correctness, each resembling its own class.

context.previous: 人若六畜首目在下，茲謂亡上，正將變更。
context.next: 二首，下不壹也；

translations.chinese: 二首，下不壹也；
translations.literal: Literal: Two heads means those below are not unified;
translations.idiomatic: Two heads means those below are not unified;

context.previous: 凡妖之作，以譴失正，各象其類。
context.next: 足多，所任邪也；

translations.chinese: 足多，所任邪也；
translations.literal: Literal: many feet means what is entrusted is deviant;
translations.idiomatic: many feet means what is entrusted is deviant;

context.previous: 二首，下不壹也；
context.next: 足少，下不勝任，或不任下也。

translations.chinese: 足少，下不勝任，或不任下也。
translations.literal: Literal: few feet means those below cannot bear responsibility, or those above do not entrust below.
translations.idiomatic: few feet means those below cannot bear responsibility, or those above do not entrust below.

context.previous: 足多，所任邪也；
context.next: 凡下體生於上，不敬也；

translations.chinese: 凡下體生於上，不敬也；
translations.literal: Literal: Whenever lower body parts grow above, it means irreverence;
translations.idiomatic: Whenever lower body parts grow above, it means irreverence;

context.previous: 足少，下不勝任，或不任下也。
context.next: 上體生於下，媟瀆也；

translations.chinese: 上體生於下，媟瀆也；
translations.literal: Literal: upper body parts growing below means desecration;
translations.idiomatic: upper body parts growing below means desecration;

context.previous: 凡下體生於上，不敬也；
context.next: 生非其類，淫亂也；

translations.chinese: 生非其類，淫亂也；
translations.literal: Literal: birth outside its kind means licentious disorder;
translations.idiomatic: birth outside its kind means licentious disorder;

context.previous: 上體生於下，媟瀆也；
context.next: 人生而大，上速成也；

translations.chinese: 人生而大，上速成也；
translations.literal: Literal: being born already large means those above mature too quickly;
translations.idiomatic: being born already large means those above mature too quickly;

context.previous: 生非其類，淫亂也；
context.next: 生而能言，好虛也。

translations.chinese: 生而能言，好虛也。
translations.literal: Literal: being born able to speak means fondness for emptiness.
translations.idiomatic: being born able to speak means fondness for emptiness.

context.previous: 人生而大，上速成也；
context.next: 群妖推此類，不改乃成凶也。」

translations.chinese: 群妖推此類，不改乃成凶也。」
translations.literal: Literal: By this kind of reasoning for all portents, if there is no reform they become full calamity."
translations.idiomatic: By this kind of reasoning for all portents, if there is no reform they become full calamity."

context.previous: 生而能言，好虛也。

translations.chinese: 景帝二年九月，膠東下密人年七十餘，生角，角有毛。
translations.literal: Literal: In ninth month of Emperor Jing year two, a man over seventy in Xiami, Jiaodong, grew horns with hair on them.
translations.idiomatic: In ninth month of Emperor Jing year two, a man over seventy in Xiami, Jiaodong, grew horns with hair on them.

context.next: 時膠東、膠西、濟南、齊四主有舉兵反謀，謀由吳王濞起，連楚、趙，凡七國。

translations.chinese: 時膠東、膠西、濟南、齊四主有舉兵反謀，謀由吳王濞起，連楚、趙，凡七國。
translations.literal: Literal: At that time the four rulers of Jiaodong, Jiaoxi, Jinan, and Qi were plotting armed rebellion; the plot began with King Pi of Wu, linking Chu and Zhao, seven states in all.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the four rulers of Jiaodong, Jiaoxi, Jinan, and Qi were plotting armed rebellion; the plot began with King Pi of Wu, linking Chu and Zhao, seven states in all.

context.previous: 景帝二年九月，膠東下密人年七十餘，生角，角有毛。
context.next: 下密，縣居四齊之中；

translations.chinese: 下密，縣居四齊之中；
translations.literal: Literal: Xiami county lay in the middle of those four Qi states;
translations.idiomatic: Xiami county lay in the middle of those four Qi states;

context.previous: 時膠東、膠西、濟南、齊四主有舉兵反謀，謀由吳王濞起，連楚、趙，凡七國。
context.next: 角，兵象，上鄉者也；

translations.chinese: 角，兵象，上鄉者也；
translations.literal: Literal: horns symbolize arms, pointing upward;
translations.idiomatic: horns symbolize arms, pointing upward;

context.previous: 下密，縣居四齊之中；
context.next: 老人，吳王象也；

translations.chinese: 老人，吳王象也；
translations.literal: Literal: the old man symbolized King of Wu;
translations.idiomatic: the old man symbolized King of Wu;

context.previous: 角，兵象，上鄉者也；
context.next: 年七十，七國象也。

translations.chinese: 年七十，七國象也。
translations.literal: Literal: age seventy symbolized the seven states.
translations.idiomatic: age seventy symbolized the seven states.

context.previous: 老人，吳王象也；
context.next: 天戒若曰，人不當生角，猶諸侯不當舉兵以鄉京師也；

translations.chinese: 天戒若曰，人不當生角，猶諸侯不當舉兵以鄉京師也；
translations.literal: Literal: Heaven's warning was as if saying: people should not grow horns, just as feudal lords should not raise troops toward the capital;
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was as if saying: people should not grow horns, just as feudal lords should not raise troops toward the capital;

context.previous: 年七十，七國象也。
context.next: 禍從老人生，七國俱敗云。

translations.chinese: 禍從老人生，七國俱敗云。
translations.literal: Literal: disaster would arise from an old man, and all seven states would be defeated.
translations.idiomatic: disaster would arise from an old man, and all seven states would be defeated.

context.previous: 天戒若曰，人不當生角，猶諸侯不當舉兵以鄉京師也；
context.next: 諸侯不寤，明年吳王先起，諸侯從之，七國俱滅。

translations.chinese: 諸侯不寤，明年吳王先起，諸侯從之，七國俱滅。
translations.literal: Literal: The feudal lords did not awaken. Next year the King of Wu rose first, the feudal lords followed, and all seven states were destroyed.
translations.idiomatic: The feudal lords did not awaken. Next year the King of Wu rose first, the feudal lords followed, and all seven states were destroyed.

context.previous: 禍從老人生，七國俱敗云。
context.next: 京房易傳曰：「冢宰專政，厥妖人生角。」

translations.chinese: 京房易傳曰：「冢宰專政，厥妖人生角。」
translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the chief minister monopolizes government, its portent is people growing horns."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the chief minister monopolizes government, its portent is people growing horns."

context.previous: 諸侯不寤，明年吳王先起，諸侯從之，七國俱滅。

translations.chinese: 成帝建始三年十月丁未，京師相驚，言大水至。
translations.literal: Literal: On dingwei day of tenth month, Jianshi year three under Emperor Cheng, the capital was alarmed by rumors that great floods were arriving.
translations.idiomatic: On dingwei day of tenth month, Jianshi year three under Emperor Cheng, the capital was alarmed by rumors that great floods were arriving.

context.next: 渭水虒上小女陳持弓年九歲，走入橫城門，入未央宮尚方掖門，殿門門衛戶者莫見，至句盾禁中而覺得。

translations.chinese: 渭水虒上小女陳持弓年九歲，走入橫城門，入未央宮尚方掖門，殿門門衛戶者莫見，至句盾禁中而覺得。
translations.literal: Literal: A nine-year-old girl of Wei-shang at Chen named Chi Gong ran into Hengcheng Gate, entered the side gate of the palace workshops in Weiyang, and none of the hall gate guards saw her. She was only noticed when she reached the internal district of the military guard.
translations.idiomatic: A nine-year-old girl of Wei-shang at Chen named Chi Gong ran into Hengcheng Gate, entered the side gate of the palace workshops in Weiyang, and none of the hall gate guards saw her. She was only noticed when she reached the internal district of the military guard.

context.previous: 成帝建始三年十月丁未，京師相驚，言大水至。
context.next: 民以水相驚者，陰氣盛也。

translations.chinese: 民以水相驚者，陰氣盛也。
translations.literal: Literal: People alarming one another about floods meant yin qi was excessive.
translations.idiomatic: People alarming one another about floods meant yin qi was excessive.

context.previous: 渭水虒上小女陳持弓年九歲，走入橫城門，入未央宮尚方掖門，殿門門衛戶者莫見，至句盾禁中而覺得。
context.next: 小女而入宮殿中者，下人將因女寵而居有宮室之象也。

translations.chinese: 小女而入宮殿中者，下人將因女寵而居有宮室之象也。
translations.literal: Literal: A little girl entering the palace halls symbolized inferiors using female favor to occupy palace chambers.
translations.idiomatic: A little girl entering the palace halls symbolized inferiors using female favor to occupy palace chambers.

context.previous: 民以水相驚者，陰氣盛也。
context.next: 名曰持弓，有似周家览弧之祥。

translations.chinese: 名曰持弓，有似周家览弧之祥。
translations.literal: Literal: Her name, Chi Gong (holding bow), resembled the Zhou omen of mulberry bow.
translations.idiomatic: Her name, Chi Gong (holding bow), resembled the Zhou omen of mulberry bow.

context.previous: 小女而入宮殿中者，下人將因女寵而居有宮室之象也。
context.next: 《易》曰：「弧矢之利，以威天下。」

translations.chinese: 《易》曰：「弧矢之利，以威天下。」
translations.literal: Literal: The Changes says: "The benefit of bow and arrow is to overawe the realm."
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says: "The benefit of bow and arrow is to overawe the realm."

context.previous: 名曰持弓，有似周家览弧之祥。
context.next: 是時，帝母王太后弟鳳始為上將，秉國政，天知其後將威天下而入宮室，故象先見也。

translations.chinese: 是時，帝母王太后弟鳳始為上將，秉國政，天知其後將威天下而入宮室，故象先見也。
translations.literal: Literal: At that time Wang Feng, younger brother of the emperor's mother Empress Dowager Wang, had just become supreme general and held state power. Heaven knew he would later dominate the realm and enter palace authority, so the sign appeared first.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Wang Feng, younger brother of the emperor's mother Empress Dowager Wang, had just become supreme general and held state power. Heaven knew he would later dominate the realm and enter palace authority, so the sign appeared first.

context.previous: 《易》曰：「弧矢之利，以威天下。」
context.next: 其後，王氏兄弟父子五侯秉權，至莽卒篡天下，蓋陳氏之後云。

translations.chinese: 其後，王氏兄弟父子五侯秉權，至莽卒篡天下，蓋陳氏之後云。
translations.literal: Literal: Afterward the Wang brothers and father-son Five Marquises held power, until Mang finally usurped the realm; likely this was the later Chen household omen.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward the Wang brothers and father-son Five Marquises held power, until Mang finally usurped the realm; likely this was the later Chen household omen.

context.previous: 是時，帝母王太后弟鳳始為上將，秉國政，天知其後將威天下而入宮室，故象先見也。
context.next: 京房易傳曰：「妖言動眾，茲謂不信，路將亡人，司馬死。」

translations.chinese: 京房易傳曰：「妖言動眾，茲謂不信，路將亡人，司馬死。」
translations.literal: Literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When demagogic words stir the masses, this is called untrustworthiness; roads will lose people, and the marshal will die."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When demagogic words stir the masses, this is called untrustworthiness; roads will lose people, and the marshal will die."

context.previous: 其後，王氏兄弟父子五侯秉權，至莽卒篡天下，蓋陳氏之後云。

translations.chinese: 成帝綏和二年八月庚申，鄭通里男子王褒衣絳衣小冠，帶劍入北司馬門殿東門，上前殿，入非常室中，解帷組結佩之，招前殿署長業等曰：「天帝令我居此。」
translations.literal: Literal: On gengshen day of eighth month, Suihe year two under Emperor Cheng, a man of Zhengtongli, Wang Bao, dressed in crimson clothes and small cap with sword at his belt, entered the eastern gate of the palace hall through North Sima Gate, went up to the front hall, entered a restricted chamber, untied curtain cords and tied them as pendants, and beckoned office chiefs such as Ye saying: "The Heavenly Emperor orders me to dwell here."
translations.idiomatic: On gengshen day of eighth month, Suihe year two under Emperor Cheng, a man of Zhengtongli, Wang Bao, dressed in crimson clothes and small cap with sword at his belt, entered the eastern gate of the palace hall through North Sima Gate, went up to the front hall, entered a restricted chamber, untied curtain cords and tied them as pendants, and beckoned office chiefs such as Ye saying: "The Heavenly Emperor orders me to dwell here."

context.next: 業等收縛考問，褒故公車大誰卒，病狂易，不自知入宮狀，下獄死。

translations.chinese: 業等收縛考問，褒故公車大誰卒，病狂易，不自知入宮狀，下獄死。
translations.literal: Literal: Ye and others seized, bound, and interrogated him. Bao was formerly a clerk of the public carriage office, mentally deranged and unaware of how he entered the palace. He was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: Ye and others seized, bound, and interrogated him. Bao was formerly a clerk of the public carriage office, mentally deranged and unaware of how he entered the palace. He was imprisoned and died.

context.previous: 成帝綏和二年八月庚申，鄭通里男子王褒衣絳衣小冠，帶劍入北司馬門殿東門，上前殿，入非常室中，解帷組結佩之，招前殿署長業等曰：「天帝令我居此。」
context.next: 是時王莽為大司馬，哀帝即位，莽乞骸骨就第，天知其必不退，故因是而見象也。

translations.chinese: 是時王莽為大司馬，哀帝即位，莽乞骸骨就第，天知其必不退，故因是而見象也。
translations.literal: Literal: At that time Wang Mang was Grand Marshal. When Emperor Ai took the throne, Mang requested retirement to his estate. Heaven knew he would not truly withdraw, and therefore showed this sign through the event.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Wang Mang was Grand Marshal. When Emperor Ai took the throne, Mang requested retirement to his estate. Heaven knew he would not truly withdraw, and therefore showed this sign through the event.

context.previous: 業等收縛考問，褒故公車大誰卒，病狂易，不自知入宮狀，下獄死。
